  "text": "Certificate verification in Envoy v1.31.2 is handled through a multi-layered architecture spanning the TLS subsystem:\n\n## Upstream (Client) Certificate Verification - Server Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a client connecting to upstream servers, it verifies the server's certificate:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/upstream_config.cc` configures the upstream TLS context using `ClientContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/client_ssl_socket.cc` creates client SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/client_context_impl.cc` sets up the client context and uses `SSL_set_verify(ssl_con.get(), SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 114 to enable server certificate verification for connections with SAN overrides\n4. **Verification**: `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc` implements custom verification via `SSL_CTX_set_custom_verify(ctx, verify_mode, customVerifyCallback)` at line 181\n\n## Downstream (Server) Certificate Verification - Client Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a server receiving connections, it verifies client certificates:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/downstream_config.cc` configures the downstream TLS context using `ServerContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/server_ssl_socket.cc` creates server SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc` initializes the server context and calls `cert_validator_->addClientValidationContext(ctx.ssl_ctx_.get(), config.requireClientCertificate())` at line 147-148\n4. **Client Validation**: `source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc` implements `addClientValidationContext()` which sets up client certificate verification. It calls `SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 528 when client certificates are required\n\n## Core Certificate Validation Implementation\n\n**Default Validator** (`source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc`):\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 56): Sets up SSL_VERIFY_PEER and creates X509_STORE with trusted CA certificates\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 481): Configures client certificate validation for server contexts\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 280): Performs actual certificate chain verification using:\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_init()`: Initializes verification context\n  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 315): Executes OpenSSL certificate verification\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_set_default()` (line 304): Sets verification mode based on `is_server` parameter\n\n**SPIFFE Validator** (`source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/spiffe/spiffe_validator.cc`):\n- Custom implementation for SPIFFE-compliant certificate validation\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 145): Returns `SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT`\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 108): Sets up client CA list for SPIFFE validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 194): Implements SPIFFE-specific verification using multiple trust bundles\n- Calls `X509_verify_cert()` (line 177) within `verifyCertChainUsingTrustBundleStore()`\n\n## Certificate Validation Context\n\n`source/common/ssl/certificate_validation_context_config_impl.cc` implements `CertificateValidationContextConfig` which encapsulates certificate validation settings including:\n- Trust chain verification mode (VERIFY_TRUST_CHAIN vs ACCEPT_UNTRUSTED)\n- CA certificate paths\n- Certificate hash verification\n- Subject Alternative Name matching\n- Certificate revocation list (CRL) paths\n\n## Utility Functions\n\n- `source/common/tls/utility.cc`: Provides `getX509VerificationErrorInfo()` to convert X509 verification errors to strings\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/utility.cc`: Provides X509_STORE manipulation utilities like `setIgnoreCertificateExpiration()`\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/san_matcher.cc`: Implements Subject Alternative Name matching validation\n\n## Abstraction Layer\n\n`source/common/tls/cert_validator/cert_validator.h` defines the `CertValidator` abstract interface with:\n- `initializeSslContexts()`: Setup SSL verification mode\n- `addClientValidationContext()`: Configure client certificate validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()`: Perform chain verification\n- Factory pattern in `factory.h` for validator creation\n\n## Socket-Level Integration\n\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_socket.cc`: Base SSL socket implementation\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_handshaker.cc`: Manages certificate validation status through `setCertificateValidationStatus()`\n- `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc`: Core implementation of `customVerifyCertChain()` that orchestrates validation"

  "text": "Certificate verification in Envoy v1.31.2 is handled through a multi-layered architecture spanning the TLS subsystem:\n\n## Upstream (Client) Certificate Verification - Server Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a client connecting to upstream servers, it verifies the server's certificate:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/upstream_config.cc` configures the upstream TLS context using `ClientContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/client_ssl_socket.cc` creates client SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/client_context_impl.cc` sets up the client context and uses `SSL_set_verify(ssl_con.get(), SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 114 to enable server certificate verification for connections with SAN overrides\n4. **Verification**: `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc` implements custom verification via `SSL_CTX_set_custom_verify(ctx, verify_mode, customVerifyCallback)` at line 181\n\n## Downstream (Server) Certificate Verification - Client Certificate Validation\n\nWhen Envoy acts as a server receiving connections, it verifies client certificates:\n\n1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/downstream_config.cc` configures the downstream TLS context using `ServerContextConfigImpl`\n2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/server_ssl_socket.cc` creates server SSL sockets\n3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc` initializes the server context and calls `cert_validator_->addClientValidationContext(ctx.ssl_ctx_.get(), config.requireClientCertificate())` at line 147-148\n4. **Client Validation**: `source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc` implements `addClientValidationContext()` which sets up client certificate verification. It calls `SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 528 when client certificates are required\n\n## Core Certificate Validation Implementation\n\n**Default Validator** (`source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc`):\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 56): Sets up SSL_VERIFY_PEER and creates X509_STORE with trusted CA certificates\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 481): Configures client certificate validation for server contexts\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 280): Performs actual certificate chain verification using:\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_init()`: Initializes verification context\n  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 315): Executes OpenSSL certificate verification\n  - `X509_STORE_CTX_set_default()` (line 304): Sets verification mode based on `is_server` parameter\n\n**SPIFFE Validator** (`source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/spiffe/spiffe_validator.cc`):\n- Custom implementation for SPIFFE-compliant certificate validation\n- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 145): Returns `SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT`\n- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 108): Sets up client CA list for SPIFFE validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 194): Implements SPIFFE-specific verification using multiple trust bundles\n- Calls `X509_verify_cert()` (line 177) within `verifyCertChainUsingTrustBundleStore()`\n\n## Certificate Validation Context\n\n`source/common/ssl/certificate_validation_context_config_impl.cc` implements `CertificateValidationContextConfig` which encapsulates certificate validation settings including:\n- Trust chain verification mode (VERIFY_TRUST_CHAIN vs ACCEPT_UNTRUSTED)\n- CA certificate paths\n- Certificate hash verification\n- Subject Alternative Name matching\n- Certificate revocation list (CRL) paths\n\n## Utility Functions\n\n- `source/common/tls/utility.cc`: Provides `getX509VerificationErrorInfo()` to convert X509 verification errors to strings\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/utility.cc`: Provides X509_STORE manipulation utilities like `setIgnoreCertificateExpiration()`\n- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/san_matcher.cc`: Implements Subject Alternative Name matching validation\n\n## Abstraction Layer\n\n`source/common/tls/cert_validator/cert_validator.h` defines the `CertValidator` abstract interface with:\n- `initializeSslContexts()`: Setup SSL verification mode\n- `addClientValidationContext()`: Configure client certificate validation\n- `doVerifyCertChain()`: Perform chain verification\n- Factory pattern in `factory.h` for validator creation\n\n## Socket-Level Integration\n\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_socket.cc`: Base SSL socket implementation\n- `source/common/tls/ssl_handshaker.cc`: Manages certificate validation status through `setCertificateValidationStatus()`\n- `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc`: Core implementation of `customVerifyCertChain()` that orchestrates validation"

    68→  "text": "Certificate verification in Envoy v1.31.2 is handled through a multi-layered architecture spanning the TLS subsystem:

## Upstream (Client) Certificate Verification - Server Certificate Validation

When Envoy acts as a client connecting to upstream servers, it verifies the server's certificate:

1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/upstream_config.cc` configures the upstream TLS context using `ClientContextConfigImpl`
2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/client_ssl_socket.cc` creates client SSL sockets
3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/client_context_impl.cc` sets up the client context and uses `SSL_set_verify(ssl_con.get(), SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 114 to enable server certificate verification for connections with SAN overrides
4. **Verification**: `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc` implements custom verification via `SSL_CTX_set_custom_verify(ctx, verify_mode, customVerifyCallback)` at line 181

## Downstream (Server) Certificate Verification - Client Certificate Validation

When Envoy acts as a server receiving connections, it verifies client certificates:

1. **Configuration**: `source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/downstream_config.cc` configures the downstream TLS context using `ServerContextConfigImpl`
2. **Socket Factory**: `source/common/tls/server_ssl_socket.cc` creates server SSL sockets
3. **Context**: `source/common/tls/server_context_impl.cc` initializes the server context and calls `cert_validator_->addClientValidationContext(ctx.ssl_ctx_.get(), config.requireClientCertificate())` at line 147-148
4. **Client Validation**: `source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc` implements `addClientValidationContext()` which sets up client certificate verification. It calls `SSL_CTX_set_verify(ctx, SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, nullptr)` at line 528 when client certificates are required

## Core Certificate Validation Implementation

**Default Validator** (`source/common/tls/cert_validator/default_validator.cc`):
- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 56): Sets up SSL_VERIFY_PEER and creates X509_STORE with trusted CA certificates
- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 481): Configures client certificate validation for server contexts
- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 280): Performs actual certificate chain verification using:
  - `X509_STORE_CTX_init()`: Initializes verification context
  - `X509_verify_cert()` (line 315): Executes OpenSSL certificate verification
  - `X509_STORE_CTX_set_default()` (line 304): Sets verification mode based on `is_server` parameter

**SPIFFE Validator** (`source/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/cert_validator/spiffe/spiffe_validator.cc`):
- Custom implementation for SPIFFE-compliant certificate validation
- `initializeSslContexts()` (line 145): Returns `SSL_VERIFY_PEER | SSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT`
- `addClientValidationContext()` (line 108): Sets up client CA list for SPIFFE validation
- `doVerifyCertChain()` (line 194): Implements SPIFFE-specific verification using multiple trust bundles
- Calls `X509_verify_cert()` (line 177) within `verifyCertChainUsingTrustBundleStore()`

## Certificate Validation Context

`source/common/ssl/certificate_validation_context_config_impl.cc` implements `CertificateValidationContextConfig` which encapsulates certificate validation settings including:
- Trust chain verification mode (VERIFY_TRUST_CHAIN vs ACCEPT_UNTRUSTED)
- CA certificate paths
- Certificate hash verification
- Subject Alternative Name matching
- Certificate revocation list (CRL) paths

## Utility Functions

- `source/common/tls/utility.cc`: Provides `getX509VerificationErrorInfo()` to convert X509 verification errors to strings
- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/utility.cc`: Provides X509_STORE manipulation utilities like `setIgnoreCertificateExpiration()`
- `source/common/tls/cert_validator/san_matcher.cc`: Implements Subject Alternative Name matching validation

## Abstraction Layer

`source/common/tls/cert_validator/cert_validator.h` defines the `CertValidator` abstract interface with:
- `initializeSslContexts()`: Setup SSL verification mode
- `addClientValidationContext()`: Configure client certificate validation
- `doVerifyCertChain()`: Perform chain verification
- Factory pattern in `factory.h` for validator creation

## Socket-Level Integration

- `source/common/tls/ssl_socket.cc`: Base SSL socket implementation
- `source/common/tls/ssl_handshaker.cc`: Manages certificate validation status through `setCertificateValidationStatus()`
- `source/common/tls/context_impl.cc`: Core implementation of `customVerifyCertChain()` that orchestrates validation"
